service.language: PHP 7.4.33 on Apache/2.4.54 (Debian), likely custom PHP

vulnerabilities.description: The id parameter accepts PHP filenames (e.g., ScriptKiddie.php) for dynamic inclusion. This pattern is commonly vulnerable to LFI/path traversal or PHP wrapper inclusion (e.g., php://filter) if whitelist/validation is incomplete. A traversal attempt returned 'Post not found', suggesting some checks, but the inclusion design remains high risk.
Responses disclose 'Server: Apache/2.4.54 (Debian)' and 'X-Powered-By: PHP/7.4.33'. PHP 7.4 is end-of-life; version disclosure aids fingerprinting and increases exposure to known issues.
Apache mod_status endpoint exists but is currently forbidden (403). If misconfigured, it can expose sensitive runtime details (requests, paths, IPs).
